justification: All pass criteria met: prototype pollution identified by name, __proto__ attack vector explained with specific payload, global Object.prototype impact explained, safe merge fix with multiple options recommended, specific curl attack example provided, safe_to_deploy false.

observations.text: Excellent explanation of the full attack chain: __proto__ → for...in iteration → recurse into Object.prototype → isAdmin set globally
Concrete curl reproduction command with realistic payload
Fix covers both immediate (hasOwnProperty + key blocklist) and strategic (schema validation with zod) approaches
Identified additional mass-assignment and deepMerge correctness bugs beyond the primary vulnerability
Rollback plan correctly notes that prototype pollution persists until process restart and polluted DB rows survive rollback
Monitoring coverage note that prototype pollution is silent and undetectable without specific instrumentation

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: prototype pollution identified by name, __proto__ attack vector explained with specific payload, global Object.prototype impact explained, safe merge fix with multiple options recommended, specific curl-based attack example provided, safe_to_deploy false.

observations.text: Excellent specific payload example: {"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}} with curl reproduction
Clearly explains the full chain: for...in iterates __proto__, typeof is object, recurses into Object.prototype, all objects affected
Fix covers both immediate guard (skip __proto__/constructor/prototype keys) and better alternative (schema validation with zod)
Bonus: identifies constructor.prototype as a secondary pollution vector
Bonus: identifies mass-assignment vulnerability as a separate critical finding
Bonus: identifies array corruption and falsy-value coercion bugs in deepMerge
Notes that rollback doesn't remediate since pollution effects may have already propagated

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: prototype pollution identified by name, __proto__ attack vector explained with specific payload, global Object.prototype impact explained, safe merge fix and whitelist recommended, specific curl reproduction example provided, safe_to_deploy false.

observations.text: Exact attack payload with curl command and __proto__/isAdmin example
Clearly explains global process-wide impact on Object.prototype
Multiple fix strategies: hasOwnProperty guard, dangerous key rejection, and replacing deepMerge with whitelist-and-spread
Identifies additional issues beyond prototype pollution (field injection, array corruption) showing thorough review
Notes that prototype pollution attacks succeed silently with no errors, making monitoring critical
